name: DISLOCATION OF CERVICAL SPINE
definition: CRANIOCERVICAL DISSOCIATION ATLANTOOCCIPITAL DISTRACTION INJURY = ATLANTO-OCCIPITAL disruption of tectorial membrane + paired alar ligaments resulting in grossly unstable injury traumatic widening of atlantoaxial interval

demographics.Age: in adults much less common than in children ← larger size of head relative to body,

dominant_finding: fluid in articular capsules, nuchal ligament, interspinous ligament
distribution: Powers ratio (assessment for anterior subluxation) = BC÷OA ratio > 1 = ratio of
discriminating_features: Powers ratio (assessment for anterior subluxation) = BC÷OA ratio > 1 = ratio of
basion-dens interval (BD) > 12 mm on X-ray / 9.5 mm on CT without traction placed on
basion-axial interval > 12 mm anterior / > 4 mm posterior to posterior axial line (PAL)
"V sign" = cranially divergent predental lines ← transverse ligament injury

general: Powers ratio (assessment for anterior subluxation) = BC÷OA ratio > 1 = ratio of
basion-dens interval (BD) > 12 mm on X-ray / 9.5 mm on CT without traction placed on
basion-axial interval > 12 mm anterior / > 4 mm posterior to posterior axial line (PAL)
"V sign" = cranially divergent predental lines ← transverse ligament injury
> 10 mm soft-tissue swelling anterior to C2 + pathologic convexity of soft tissues (80%)
blood in region of tectorial membrane + alar ligaments
condylar fracture ± fracture extension through hypoglossal canal (for cranial nerve XII)
widening / incongruity of articulation between occipital condyles + lateral masses of C1
MR: fluid in articular capsules, nuchal ligament, interspinous ligament
subluxation with enlargement of predental space to
increased signal intensity of spinal cord

clinical.Cx: (1)   Injury to caudal cranial nn. + upper 3 cervical nerves
